title: Foot numbness
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.rationale: Exact phrase located in inspected full source.

source.title: Suspected neurological conditions: recognition and referral
source.population: Adults aged over 16 years with suspected neurological conditions.
source.setting: Initial assessment and referral from primary or community care.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected adult dizziness, vertigo, limb weakness, sensory symptoms, and tremor referral features; not applicable to children and not directly validated for UAE pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.10.1–1.10.13 — sensory symptoms including tingling or numbness in adults

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports sudden unilateral numbness, rapidly progressive symmetrical sensory change with weakness or imbalance, recurrent sensory episodes, distal sensory distribution, reflex and gait context, selected metabolic tests, radicular symptoms, bowel or bladder context, and clinician-led referral.

assessment.text: Supports sudden unilateral numbness, rapidly progressive symmetrical sensory change with weakness or imbalance, recurrent sensory episodes, distal sensory distribution, reflex and gait context, selected metabolic tests, radicular symptoms, bowel or bladder context, and clinician-led referral.
assessment.rationale: The exact adult sensory-symptom section supports onset, distribution, unilateral or symmetrical pattern, progression, weakness, imbalance, gait, reflex, back or radicular, bowel or bladder, metabolic-test, and referral context without assigning neuropathy. The exact neurological section supports recording existing metabolic, neurological, or imaging results and clinician-entered follow-up or referral status without generating tests.

source.title: Suspected neurological conditions: recognition and referral
source.population: Adults aged over 16 years with suspected neurological conditions.
source.setting: Initial assessment and referral from primary or community care.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected adult dizziness, vertigo, limb weakness, sensory symptoms, and tremor referral features; not applicable to children and not directly validated for UAE pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.10.1–1.10.13 — sensory symptoms including tingling or numbness in adults

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports sudden unilateral numbness, rapidly progressive symmetrical sensory change with weakness or imbalance, recurrent sensory episodes, distal sensory distribution, reflex and gait context, selected metabolic tests, radicular symptoms, bowel or bladder context, and clinician-led referral.

assessment.text: Supports documenting age-appropriate review, bilateral skin and sensory examination, perfusion, ulceration, callus, infection or inflammation, deformity, gangrene, Charcot features, and clinician-entered risk classification without calculating it.
assessment.rationale: The exact diabetic-foot section supports clinician-performed bilateral skin, sensory, perfusion, ulcer, callus, infection, deformity, gangrene, and Charcot assessment only when diabetes and examination context apply.

source.title: Diabetic foot problems: prevention and management
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE adaptation
source.population: Children, young people, and adults with diabetes, with age-specific foot-assessment recommendations.
source.setting: Diabetes foot-risk assessment, active foot-problem documentation, multidisciplinary pathways, and follow-up.
source.exclusions: Exact for clinician examination of both feet, neuropathy, ischaemia, ulceration, callus, infection or inflammation, deformity, gangrene, Charcot context, risk classification already assigned, and foot-review documentation. It does not auto-classify risk or generate referral or treatment.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.6 — frequency, bilateral foot examination, and clinician risk stratification

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting age-appropriate review, bilateral skin and sensory examination, perfusion, ulceration, callus, infection or inflammation, deformity, gangrene, Charcot features, and clinician-entered risk classification without calculating it.
